regime: Risk-On Reversal (NQ Reclaims 30,000, Gold Flips Short)
summary: Friday's hot-PCE risk-off fully reversed: equities ripped and the haven bid drained out of gold. A failed morning breakdown set the trap. MNQ broke its opening-range low near 29,540 in the first half hour, squeezed the early shorts, and marked up all session to close 29,995.5 (+2.41%), reclaiming the 30,000 round number and tagging a 30,069 RTH high. MES confirmed: +1.29% to 7,493, clearing 7,500 intraday (7,505 high) and aligning the equity complex long for the first time since the PCE scare. Both TSMOM-20 legs now sit above rising 1hr trend stops (MNQ +340, MES +55). The commodity side mirrored the flip: gold gave back its fresh highs and lost its 1hr stop to close 4,059 (-1.08%, flipped short), while crude reclaimed its stop and flipped back long (+0.27%). Friday's fragile long became a healthy trend. The one caution: this prints into Jun 30 quarter-end rebalancing and a holiday-shortened jobs week.

setups.name: TSMOM-20 1hr (LONG, healthy)
setups.entry: Already long; the system stays long while hourly closes hold above the 29,655 stop
setups.t1: 30,000 psych held, then the +2s 30,219 and today's 30,069 high
setups.bias: In-habitat trend long, a clear upgrade from Friday's fragile read. No chase at 30,000 into thin quarter-end liquidity; let pullbacks toward AVWAP 29,900 define risk. No adds, no early exit; the stop decides.

setups.name: ES Alignment Watch (MES)
setups.entry: An hourly close that holds above 7,500 sustains the equity-complex long
setups.bias: MES cleared 7,500 intraday but hourly closes settled 7,493, just under. A clean hold of 7,500 keeps NQ and ES aligned; a loss of 7,438 turns ES the laggard again.

event.name: No scheduled first-tier US release (quarter-end Monday; the data cluster is Jun 30 to Jul 2)
event.consensus: Quiet Monday data slate. The focus was positioning into Jun 30 quarter-end rebalancing and the holiday-shortened jobs week, not a single print.
event.actual: No first-tier US release. Price action was a clean risk-on reversal of Friday's hot-PCE fade, led by mega-cap tech.
event.surprise: None scheduled. The move was flow and positioning: an early failed breakdown that squeezed shorts into quarter-end.
event.nqReaction: MNQ failed its downside opening-range break (29,543 at 14:00 UTC), reversed, and trended up all session to close 29,995.5 (+2.41%), reclaiming 30,000 and tagging 30,069. The early short got squeezed.
event.crossAsset: MES +1.29% to 7,493 (cleared 7,500 intraday, hourly closes just under). MGC -1.08% to 4,059, gave back fresh highs and flipped its 1hr stop short. MCL +0.27% to 70.45, reclaimed its stop and flipped back long. Risk-on broad; gold the lone loser.

observe.name: GAP_NQ (gap fade)
observe.note: Weekend gap up was absorbed and extended, not faded. No clean gap-fade setup; the trend-day tape overrides the fade into quarter-end.

observe.name: TSMOM252LO_NQ (long-only 252)
observe.note: The longer-horizon long re-strengthens as price reclaims 30,000 and pulls well clear of the short-horizon 29,655 stop.

observe.name: ES confirmation
observe.note: MES aligned the equity complex long for the first time since PCE, clearing 7,500 intraday; hourly closes at 7,493 are a hair short of a full 7,500 hold.

risk.throttleState: REDUCED (upgraded from DEFENSIVE). TSMOM-20 is a healthy in-habitat long, +340 over a rising stop with ES confirming, but quarter-end and the jobs week argue against full NORMAL sizing.
risk.ddProximity: Comfortable. The 340-pt MNQ buffer above the stop removes the single-bad-hour risk that defined Friday. Drawdown proximity is low.
risk.dailyLossBudget: Intact. The binding risk now is overtrading a thin quarter-end tape, not a gap to the stop.
risk.sizing: Step size up modestly from Friday's defensive floor, but hold below full size into Jun 30 rebalancing. Respect the 29,655 stop; do not chase 30,000 on first touch. The long-only equity stack takes neither the gold short nor the crude long.
risk.note: Week ahead: Jun 30 quarter-end rebalancing (Chicago PMI 9:45 ET, CB Consumer Confidence 10:00 ET), ISM Manufacturing Wed Jul 1 (10:00 ET), and the June jobs report Thu Jul 2 (8:30 ET, pulled forward ahead of the Jul 3 half-day before Independence Day).

psychology: The trade you want to take at the high is usually the trade that just paid someone else. Today rewarded the trader who waited. The morning breakdown looked like Friday continuing, and whoever shorted the opening-range break got squeezed for 400 points; whoever chased 30,000 on the first tag bought the high tick of the hour. The edge was in the middle: a failed breakdown plus an AVWAP reclaim, sized and patient. As the tape rips into quarter-end, the discipline is to let price come back to your level instead of chasing the move that already happened. A trend you have to chase is a trend you will mismanage.

fullReport.title: Market Regime Snapshot (6-Axis)
fullReport.html:
AxisStateConfidence
DirectionRisk-ON, broad equity uptrend. MNQ +2.41% reclaimed 30,000 and sits +340 over a RISING TSMOM-20 stop; MES +1.29% cleared 7,500 (7,505 high), also long and rising. Gold FLIPPED short (-1.08%, below 4,099); crude flipped back long (+0.27%).HIGH
VolatilityExpansion, but directional. MNQ ATR(14) 150 (down from Friday's 196). Wide RTH range (29,274 to 30,069, ~795 pts) yet one-directional after the morning flush. A trend day, not chop.MODERATE
SessionTrend day up after a FAILED morning breakdown. MNQ broke its OR low at 14:00 UTC (29,543) and MES at 14:05 (7,440); both reversed and marked up to close near RTH highs.HIGH
InventoryNet long / short-covering. Early OR-low shorts trapped and squeezed; closes pinned near highs (MNQ 29,995.5 vs 30,069 high; MES 7,493 vs 7,505).MODERATE
EventPRE_EVENT / quarter-end. Tomorrow Jun 30 = final quarter-end rebalancing (Chicago PMI, CB Consumer Confidence). Then Jul 1 ISM Mfg, Jul 2 June jobs (pulled forward before the Jul 3 half-day).HIGH
LiquidityThinning into quarter-end and the July 4 holiday week. Jun 30 rebalancing flows can distort the tape; size accordingly.MODERATE

Cross-asset: a clean mirror reversal of Friday. The hot-PCE risk-off fade fully unwound: equities ripped (MNQ +2.41%, MES +1.29%), gold sold its fresh highs and flipped short (-1.08%), and crude firmed back above its 1hr stop (+0.27%). Risk-on is broad and ES-confirmed; the split now runs equities-up / gold-down.

fullReport.title: NY Opening Range / COMPASS (MNQ)
fullReport.html: NY Opening Range / COMPASS (MNQ). OR (13:30 to 13:44 UTC) 29,557 to 29,795, width 238. Break DOWN at 14:00 UTC (close 29,543 below the OR low) that FAILED: price reversed back inside the OR within the hour, reclaimed AVWAP, and trended up the rest of the session to a 30,069 high. The failed downside break was the trap that fueled the squeeze, and the same pattern marked MES (downside break 14:05, reversed). Failed-breakdown was the COMPASS tell of the day.

fullReport.title: Momentum Breakout (Regime Filter)
fullReport.html: Momentum Breakout (Regime Filter). Long signal confirmed after the reversal. Once price reclaimed AVWAP and the 29,795 OR high on volume, the regime filter turned constructive: higher highs above VWAP, RVOL building into the trend rather than front-loaded at the open. The higher-quality long was the failed-breakdown reclaim, not a fresh range break. Caveat: do not extrapolate a trend day into thin quarter-end flows.

fullReport.title: AVWAP Deviation Band Reversal (MNQ)
fullReport.html: AVWAP Deviation Band Reversal (MNQ). Center 29,900, sigma 159. Bands: +1s 30,060, +2s 30,219, +3s 30,379; -1s 29,741, -2s 29,582, -3s 29,422. Price closed 29,995.5, between AVWAP and the +1s band, extended up but not stretched to a reversion trigger. The session low 29,274 tagged below the -2s intraday and reversed, which was the high-quality long. Re-qualifies for a fade only on a clean +2s/-2s tag with an exhaustion bar and a center reclaim, half size. AVWAP/bands are price-based this run (volume unavailable; Alphatrends indicator not loaded), disclaimed accordingly.

fullReport.html:

Hold the long while hourly closes stay above 29,655; a 2-close break stops the system out. A clear upgrade from Friday's borderline read.

fullReport.title: Yesterday's Map: Marked (Jun 26 map, checked on the Jun 29 session)
fullReport.html:
#Map rowResultMeasured
1MNQ holds the 29,260 ATR stop and reclaims AVWAP 29,472 / the 29,500 psych on hourly closesTRIGGERED, PLAYED OUTHeld 29,260 (RTH low 29,274, 14 pts above), reclaimed AVWAP and 29,500 decisively, then ran past the +2s 29,670 and the 29,717 RTH high all the way to 30,069. The repair scenario played out in full.
2MNQ loses the 29,260 stop on a 2-close basisNOT TRIGGEREDNever lost 29,260; the session low was 29,274. The stops-out scenario did not occur.
3MNQ chops between 29,260 and AVWAP 29,472 without resolvingNOT TRIGGEREDPrice dipped into the zone early but resolved decisively up rather than ranging; no quarter-end chop materialized.
4MES reclaims and holds 7,454 then 7,500 on hourly closesTRIGGERED, PLAYED OUTReclaimed and held 7,454 firmly and tagged a 7,505 high; ES confirmed the long and the complex aligned. The nuance: hourly closes settled 7,493 to 7,497, a hair under a full 7,500 hold.
5MGC holds its 4,054 stop and MCL stays below its 70.52 stopNOT TRIGGEREDNeither leg held: gold lost 4,054 (RTH low 4,043) and flipped short, while crude reclaimed 70.52 and flipped long. The risk-off-persists scenario was voided; the mirror reversal happened instead.

fullReport.title: Tomorrow's Map (for Tuesday, June 30, 2026)
fullReport.html:
Map for Tuesday, June 30, 2026 (quarter-end). Filters: Tuesday is not Mon or Wed, with no CPI or FOMC, so all four playbooks are eligible (BIR, HOF, MID, BNC). The HOF 12:00 to 14:00 ET dead zone and the MID 9:30 to 10:30 ET window still apply, and quarter-end rebalancing can distort the open. Calendar: Jun 30 is quarter-end, with MNI Chicago PMI 9:45 ET and CB Consumer Confidence 10:00 ET. The week then builds to ISM Manufacturing Wed Jul 1 (10:00 ET) and the June jobs report Thu Jul 2 (8:30 ET, pulled forward ahead of the Jul 3 half-day before Independence Day). All levels from today's 1hr chart.

yesterdaysMapScore.note: The risk-on reversal day. Row 1 (hold 29,260 + reclaim AVWAP/29,500) TRIGGERED and PLAYED OUT in full, running past 29,717 to 30,069. Row 4 (MES reclaims 7,454 then 7,500) TRIGGERED and PLAYED OUT, ES confirmed and tagged 7,505 (closes settled 7,493, a hair under 7,500). Row 2 (2-close loss of 29,260) NOT TRIGGERED, low 29,274. Row 3 (chop 29,260 to 29,472) NOT TRIGGERED, resolved up. Row 5 (commodities hold their Friday regimes) NOT TRIGGERED, both flipped and risk-on returned.
